title: Shadow Toss — 50 Reps
description: Stand at the baseline without a racket. Toss the ball 50 times, catching it at the apex each time without moving your feet. Goal: the ball lands on the same spot on your palm every rep. Track drift visually.

title: Coin-on-Racket Drill
description: Place a coin on the strings. Toss and swing — if the coin falls, you dropped your gaze before contact. Do 20 serves focusing only on watching the ball until after contact.

title: 3-Bounce Reset Protocol
description: Before every second serve, enforce exactly 3 ball bounces and a full breath. Use a metronome app set to your preferred tempo. This rebuilds the same physical routine rhythm as your first serve.

title: Toss-Arc Wall Target
description: Stand arm's length from a fence or wall. Mark a target at trophy height. Toss to that target 30 times without hitting the wall. This constrains your toss to a straight vertical path.

title: Slow-Motion Trophy Freeze
description: Serve at 30% speed, stopping at the trophy position for 2 seconds before accelerating through contact. Builds kinesthetic awareness of the position your best serves share.

title: Flat T-Serve Targets
description: Place two cones at the T on each side. Hit 20 flat first serves per cone. Count how many land within a racket-length of the T. Builds precision from your most aggressive toss position.

title: Kick-Serve Toss Placement
description: For kick serves, the toss should land slightly behind your head if you let it drop. Practice tossing 20 times and letting the ball drop — it should land 6–8 inches behind your baseline foot. Confirm with video.

title: Slice-Serve Toss Drift
description: Slice serve toss should drift slightly to your non-dominant side. Mark a spot on the baseline one racket-head width to your right (for right-handers). Toss to that spot 20 times to groove the placement.
